subject: Ecology
title: Wetland Restoration
description: A quick win: understand Wetland Restoration.

hook.text: Restoring a drained wetland starts with one simple step: let the water come back.

buildup.text: Blocking drainage ditches and removing levees allows natural flooding to resume.

discovery.text: Once water returns, wetland plants and animals often recolonize within a few years.

twist.text: Full ecosystem recovery takes decades—soil biology and species interactions rebuild slowly.

climax.text: The Kissimmee River restoration in Florida unchanneled 70 km of river and revived vast marshes.

punchline.text: Return the water and nature does the rest.

quiz.question: What is the first step in restoring a drained wetland?
options: Allowing water to return by removing drainage
